Interesting! 25 cells changed, gameplay changed, and this is a NEW state (node 8). Looking at the image:
- The purple diamond ring at top now has a GRAY border (instead of purple)
- The white-bordered dot at bottom is now at (59,27) with white border
- The magenta dot is still at (40,33)
- The trail is still there
So clicking the gray-bordered dot from the initial state:
1. Changed the purple diamond ring's border to gray
2. Moved the white-bordered dot from (21,39) to (59,27)
This is very different from what happened before! Before, when the white-bordered was already at (53,28), clicking (59,27) just swapped borders. But from the initial state, clicking (59,27) moved the white-bordered all the way to the end AND changed the ring's color.
Now let me see the current state and figure out what to do next. The white-bordered is at the end (59,27). The ring at top is now gray-bordered. Let me check the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Key objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 25 and n['color'] not in ('B', 'g'):
        center_row = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} ~({center_row},{center_col}) children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
